content_en:
Any group of people who want to work for Islam must have their share and provision of endurance. Otherwise, they should leave this path to those who are more deserved of it, namely the truthful and patient believers. In his biography of the Prophet , Ibn Kathir wrote, " Messenger of Allah spent ten years in Makkah, preaching to the people in their homes and in public gatherings and saying to them, 'Who would shelter me and support me until I convey the Message of Allah, and [such a person] will receive the [reward of) Paradise.' But he did not find anyone to shelter and support him, until the Ansaar (or the helpers) believed him and embraced Islam. Seventy-two of them came to meet him at a place known as Al- 'Aqabah. The Prophet promised them Paradise if they accepted his conditions.

The youngest of them, As'ad bin Zurarah, stood up and said, 'Slow down, O people of Yathrib 126. We did not tire our camels [on the way here] for him without knowing that he is the Messenger of Allah . Supporting him today will be in defiance of all the Arabs, it will result in the killing of your best men and the blow of swords upon you. Either you endure this, in which case take his offer and expect your reward from Allah, or if you are unsure of yourselves and afraid, in which case abandon him to his fate. So tell him your choice now, for either way he will vouch for you before Allah.

' They said, `Stand aside O As'ad! By Allah, we will never leave this allegiance to him, nor will we cancel it.'" The Muslim Jama'ah that wishes to support this faith and aspires after Paradise by doing so must have sufficient endurance to enable it to fulfil this commitment, assume the responsibility and confront the enemies. If, however, it does not know what endurance is, cannot stand what it entails and has no intention to exercise it, it must admit to its failings and step aside. Doing so might excuse it before Allah and is better for Islam than going forth carrying the banner and then soon turning on its heels, drooping the banner, only to cause great tumult and oppression in the land. The Messenger of Allah said, "And know that victory comes with endurance." 127 So whoever wants victory must show patience and endurance.

Allah (SWT) says (which means), "And the fair promise of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel because they had patience and constancy." [Surah Al-A'raaf (7), Ayah 137.] 126 Yathrib was the name of the city which later came to known as Al-Madeenah. 127 Tirmidhi. 125 guidance under Our Command, so long as they persevered with patience and continued to have faith in Our signs." [Surah As-Sajdah (32), Ayah 24.] The Muslim Jama'ah, therefore, must teach endurance to its members, train them and cloak them with it. The Prophet said, "He who tries to endure is assisted by Allah in his endeavour." The Jama'ah must teach its members that endurance is a vehicle that must be alighted in order for them to attain the honour and success that are the dream and aspiration of the Muslim Ummah. It must also teach them that endurance is the conduit to all the degrees and stations of faith. Those who cannot endure will not achieve anywhere. Whoever wants to acquire some knowledge must patiently learn and memorise.

Whoever aspires to the higher stations of piety will not achieve this aim without patience and endurance. Likewise, those who wish to rid themselves of sinful habits and conduct and cleanse their hearts and limbs, need patience and endurance to carry them through this process. Indeed, patience and endurance is the key to all good and the light of all guidance. Allah (SWT) says (which means), "As for those who wage Jihad in Our Cause, We will surely guide them to Our Paths (i. e. Allah's religion).

And verily, Allah is with those who do right." [Surah Al-'Ankabut (29), Ayah 69.] Furthermore, maintaining any of the stations thus achieved requires additional endurance. Those who cease to strive and abandon endurance will be stripped of their gains, their status will be reduced and their progress reversed. The need for patience and endurance is especially important for those who work in the cause of Allah. Preachers need patient perseverance so as not to lose hope when they see that no-one has responded to their call. They also need to endure any reaction to their activities that might be harmful to them.

Educationalists need patient perseverance to deal with the mistakes and inattention of their charges. The leaders need it to lead those who are not inclined to obedience. Those who undertake enjoining good and forbidding evil need it to I endure harm. And those who undertake Jihad are without doubt most in need for endurance in order to keep up the struggle under all conditions and in all circumstances, "Fighting [Jihad] is ordained upon you though you dislike it." [Surah AlBaqarah (2), Ayah 216.] Endurance is more needed today than ever before. We live with our dawah in countries controlled by our enemies whom we aim to remove and replace their rule with that of our Shariah.

They will frequently attack us and seek to destroy us, and without endurance they will undoubtedly succeed in uprooting our dawah and wiping us off the face of this earth. Endurance is not what many people today think it is, namely not speaking up for the truth and making compromises with falsehood. This is in fact no more than humiliation, degradation and disgrace, and has nothing to do with endurance. The endurance which our Shariah commands us to observe is of three categories. a. Endurance in obeying Allah and His Messenger : While the self inclines to comfort and inaction, religious obligations entail a degree of hardship that varies from one commission to the next.

Jihad, for instance, 126 making wudu' with cold water in winter is harder than making it in summer. The degree of effort also varies from one individual to the other: those who are closer to their homes and families find emigration extremely taxing; and those with a hardened heart find prayers laborious, but Jihad remains the most exacting of absolutely all acts of worship. This is the reason why some said, as the Quran recounts (which means), "Our Lord! Why have You ordered for us fighting? Would that You had granted us respite for a short period?" [Surah An-Nisa (4), Ayah 77.] This means that every Muslim needs endurance to compel his self into obedience, for patience is needed before performing any act of obedience, to bring the self to start it and be sincere in devoting it to Allah.

It is also needed while doing the act to make the self see it through in the best possible way. It is also needed after completion of the act to prevent the heart from admiring the act of obedience and the tongue from boastfully talking about it. b. Endurance in avoiding the disobedience of Allah and His Messenger : This task is more difficult and demanding. For the human self, as the Quran said, is inclined to evil and wishes for it, and Satan entices and encourages the servant to commit the sin. Those who habitually indulge in sinful deeds advocate them and invite the faithful to join in, while society does not frown upon vice nor criticise the sinful.

To the contrary, it sometimes praises the sinful because of the general corruption in which it wallows. So how can the limbs give succumb to the call of disobedience? Indeed, it is endurance which prevents the limbs from committing the sin. We definitely need the type of patience and endurance showed by Prophet Yusuf when he resisted the advances of Al-Aziz's wife, saying (which means), "Verily, he who fears Allah with obedience to Him and is patient, then surely, Allah makes not the reward of those who do right to be lost." [Surah Yusuf (12), Ayah 90.] The Muslim Jama'ah is in need of teaching this type of patience and endurance to its members, for we will surely be defeated if we are disobedient. 'Umar bin AlKhattab spoke the truth in his instructions to the army commander, Sa'd bin Abee Waqqas when he said to him, "I command you and your soldiers to observe Taqwa which is the best preparation against the enemy and the strongest weapon of war.

I command you and your soldiers to be more on guard against sins than against the enemy, for the sins of [your] army should be feared more than the enemy. Indeed, Muslims only triumph by the sinning of their enemy against Allah, for otherwise we would not have the strength and advantage over them. Our numbers are no match for them and our preparation do not compare. If we equal them in sins, they will prevail over us through their physical strength. If we cannot defeat them through our piety, we will not defeat them through our strength." He also said.

"And do not say that our enemy is more evil than we are, and that Allah will not send them against us even if we sin. For so many are those against whom Allah sent a people who are more evil than them, as happened with the Jews when Allah (SWT) sent against them the disbelievers from amongst the Magians who entered the very innermost parts of their homes, and it was a promise completely fulfilled." 127 and persevere in their resistance to the inequity and oppression of people, and how to detest and abhor this evil quality. For if they do not learn to dissociate themselves from injustice, those of them who might, in the future, be in positions of power and responsibility might then succumb to the temptation of oppressing others. Another great vice and abhorrent act of disobedience which members of the Muslim Jama'ah must avoid is the love of leadership and influence, for so often has the struggle over leadership split the Muslim community and weakened its resolve. c. Enduring trials and tribulations: It is necessary that every Muslim must exercise this type of endurance, for otherwise he will rebel against the Divine Destiny, thus committing a major sin, "Do people think that they will be left alone because they say, 'We believe', and that they will not be tested?

And We indeed tested those who were before them. And Allah will certainly make fit] known [the truth of] those who are true, and will certainly make [it] known [the falsehood of] those who are liars [although Allah knows all that before putting them to test]." [Surah Al-'Ankabut (29), Ayah 2-3.] These tests are important for separating between the good and the bad, and casting the bad away from the ranks of the caravan; the caravan of the people of truth and endurance. Prevailing in this life is no easy task, for it implies the carrying of the burden of ruling the people by the tenets of this religion of Islam. ' This is why the laws that Allah established in the universe stipulates that only those entitled to occupy the positions of leadership should be allowed to govern. Trials and tribulations are, therefore, indispensable to distinguish the wicked from the good.

Those who survive these tests are those worthy of authority and power, "Allah will not leave the believers in the state in which you are now, until He distinguishes the wicked from the good." [Surah Ale-Imran (3), Ayah 179.] "In order that Allah may distinguish the wicked (disbelievers, polytheists, evil-doers) from the good (the believers), put the wicked one on another, heap them together, and cast them into Hell." [Surah Al-Anfal (8), Ayah 37.] The Muslim Jama'ah must preach endurance to its members if it is to deserve power. Day and night it must preach to them what Moses used to preach to his people (which means), "Seek help in Allah and be patient. Verily, the earth is Allah's. He gives it as a heritage to Whom He will of His servants, and the [blessed] end is (best) for the righteous." [Surah Al-A'raaf (7), Ayah 128.] The Muslim Jama'ah must teach them thus, "Or think you that you will enter Paradise without such [trials] as came to those who passed away before you? They were afflicted with severe poverty and ailments and were so shaken that even the Messenger and those 128 Certainly, the help of Allah is near!" [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayah 214.] It must rear them to be true men, so that when the testing times come, they say what true men say (which means), "This is what Allah and His Messenger had promised us, and Allah and His Messenger had spoken the truth." [Surah Al-Ahzaab (33), Ayah 22.] Only such people are deemed qualified to carry the trust of this faith.

Their enemies will soon be discharged as fire discharges impurities from smelted metal. Patiently enduring hardships and calamities require the following:
content_id:
Setiap kelompok orang yang ingin bekerja untuk Islam harus memiliki bagian dan bekal kesabaran mereka. Jika tidak, mereka harus meninggalkan jalan ini bagi mereka yang lebih berhak, yaitu orang-orang beriman yang jujur dan sabar. Dalam biografinya tentang Nabi ﷺ, Ibn Katsir menulis, "Rasulullah ﷺ menghabiskan sepuluh tahun di Makkah, berdakwah kepada orang-orang di rumah-rumah mereka dan di pertemuan-pertemuan publik dan berkata kepada mereka, 'Siapa yang akan melindungiku dan mendukungku hingga aku menyampaikan Pesan Allah ﷻ, dan orang seperti itu akan mendapatkan (pahala) Surga.' Tetapi beliau tidak menemukan siapa pun yang melindungi dan mendukungnya, hingga kaum Anshar (para penolong) mempercayainya dan memeluk Islam.

Tujuh puluh dua dari mereka datang menemuinya di tempat yang dikenal sebagai Al-'Aqabah. Nabi ﷺ menjanjikan Surga kepada mereka jika mereka menerima syarat-syaratnya. Yang termuda dari mereka, As'ad bin Zurarah (ra), berdiri dan berkata, 'Perlahan, wahai penduduk Yatsrib. Kami tidak melelahkan unta kami (dalam perjalanan kemari) untuknya tanpa mengetahui bahwa beliau adalah Rasulullah ﷺ. Mendukungnya hari ini akan menentang semua bangsa Arab, akan mengakibatkan terbunuhnya orang-orang terbaik kalian dan sabetan pedang atas kalian. Baik kalian bersabar terhadap ini, dalam hal ini terimalah tawarannya dan harapkan pahala kalian dari Allah ﷻ, atau jika kalian tidak yakin tentang diri kalian sendiri dan takut, dalam hal ini tinggalkan beliau dengan nasibnya. Maka katakanlah pilihanmu sekarang, karena bagaimanapun beliau akan menjamin kalian di hadapan Allah ﷻ.' Mereka berkata, 'Menyingkirlah, wahai As'ad! Demi Allah, kami tidak akan pernah meninggalkan baiat ini kepadanya, dan kami tidak akan membatalkannya.'"

Jama'ah Muslim yang ingin mendukung iman ini dan mendambakan Surga dengan melakukannya harus memiliki kesabaran yang cukup untuk memungkinkannya memenuhi komitmen ini, mengambil tanggung jawab, dan menghadapi para musuh. Namun, jika ia tidak tahu apa itu kesabaran, tidak tahan dengan apa yang dituntutnya, dan tidak berniat untuk melaksanakannya, ia harus mengakui kegagalannya dan menyingkir. Melakukan hal itu mungkin memaafkannya di hadapan Allah ﷻ dan lebih baik bagi Islam daripada maju membawa panji kemudian segera berbalik, menjatuhkan panji, hanya untuk menimbulkan keributan besar dan penindasan di muka bumi. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Dan ketahuilah bahwa kemenangan datang bersama kesabaran." [HR. Tirmidzi.] Jadi barangsiapa yang menginginkan kemenangan harus menunjukkan kesabaran dan ketabahan.

Allah ﷻ berfirman (yang artinya), "Dan telah sempurnalah perkataan Tuhanmu yang baik (sebagai janji) untuk Bani Israil disebabkan kesabaran mereka." [Surah Al-A'raf (7), Ayat 137.] "Dan Kami jadikan di antara mereka itu pemimpin-pemimpin yang memberi petunjuk dengan perintah Kami ketika mereka sabar. Dan adalah mereka meyakini ayat-ayat Kami." [Surah As-Sajdah (32), Ayat 24.] Jama'ah Muslim, oleh karena itu, harus mengajarkan kesabaran kepada anggota-anggotanya, melatih mereka dan menyelimuti mereka dengannya. Nabi ﷺ bersabda, "Barangsiapa yang berusaha untuk bersabar, Allah ﷻ akan membantunya dalam usahanya."

Jama'ah harus mengajarkan anggota-anggotanya bahwa kesabaran adalah kendaraan yang harus dinaiki agar mereka dapat mencapai kehormatan dan keberhasilan yang merupakan impian dan cita-cita umat Muslim. Ia juga harus mengajarkan mereka bahwa kesabaran adalah jalan menuju semua derajat dan kedudukan iman. Mereka yang tidak bisa bersabar tidak akan mencapai apa-apa. Barangsiapa yang ingin mendapatkan ilmu harus bersabar dalam belajar dan menghafal. Barangsiapa yang mendambakan kedudukan yang lebih tinggi dari ketakwaan tidak akan mencapai tujuan ini tanpa kesabaran dan ketabahan.

Demikian pula, mereka yang ingin membebaskan diri dari kebiasaan dan perilaku yang berdosa serta membersihkan hati dan anggota tubuh mereka, membutuhkan kesabaran dan ketabahan untuk membawa mereka melalui proses ini. Memang, kesabaran dan ketabahan adalah kunci dari semua kebaikan dan cahaya dari semua petunjuk. Allah ﷻ berfirman (yang artinya), "Dan orang-orang yang berjihad untuk (mencari keridhaan) Kami, benar-benar akan Kami tunjukkan kepada mereka jalan-jalan Kami. Dan sesungguhnya Allah benar-benar beserta orang-orang yang berbuat baik." [Surah Al-'Ankabut (29), Ayat 69.]

Selanjutnya, mempertahankan kedudukan yang telah dicapai memerlukan kesabaran tambahan. Mereka yang berhenti berjuang dan meninggalkan kesabaran akan kehilangan pencapaian mereka, status mereka akan berkurang, dan kemajuan mereka akan berbalik. Kebutuhan akan kesabaran dan ketabahan sangat penting bagi mereka yang bekerja di jalan Allah ﷻ. Para pendakwah membutuhkan kesabaran yang tekun agar tidak putus harapan ketika mereka melihat tidak ada yang merespons seruan mereka. Mereka juga perlu bersabar terhadap reaksi apa pun atas kegiatan mereka yang mungkin berbahaya bagi mereka.

Para pendidik membutuhkan kesabaran yang tekun untuk menghadapi kesalahan dan kelalaian orang-orang yang mereka bimbing. Para pemimpin membutuhkannya untuk memimpin mereka yang tidak cenderung patuh. Mereka yang melaksanakan amar ma'ruf nahi munkar membutuhkannya untuk menanggung bahaya. Dan mereka yang melaksanakan Jihad tanpa keraguan paling membutuhkan kesabaran untuk melanjutkan perjuangan dalam semua kondisi dan semua keadaan, "Diwajibkan atas kamu berperang, padahal berperang itu adalah sesuatu yang kamu benci." [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayat 216.]

Kesabaran lebih dibutuhkan hari ini daripada sebelumnya. Kita hidup dengan dakwah kita di negara-negara yang dikuasai oleh musuh-musuh kita yang kita bertujuan untuk menyingkirkan dan menggantikan pemerintahan mereka dengan Syariat kita. Mereka akan sering menyerang kita dan berusaha menghancurkan kita, dan tanpa kesabaran mereka pasti akan berhasil mencabut dakwah kita dan menghapus kita dari muka bumi ini.

Kesabaran bukanlah seperti yang banyak orang pikirkan hari ini, yaitu tidak berbicara untuk kebenaran dan membuat kompromi dengan kebatilan. Ini sebenarnya tidak lebih dari kehinaan, degradasi, dan aib, dan tidak ada hubungannya dengan kesabaran. Kesabaran yang diperintahkan Syariat kita untuk diamalkan terbagi menjadi tiga kategori:

a. Sabar dalam menaati Allah ﷻ dan Rasul-Nya ﷺ: Sementara jiwa cenderung kepada kenyamanan dan kemalasan, kewajiban-kewajiban agama menuntut tingkat kesulitan yang bervariasi dari satu amalan ke yang lainnya. Jihad, misalnya, adalah yang paling berat dari semua tindakan ibadah. Berwudhu dengan air dingin di musim dingin lebih sulit daripada di musim panas. Tingkat usaha juga bervariasi dari satu individu ke individu lain: mereka yang lebih dekat dengan rumah dan keluarga mereka merasa emigrasi sangat melelahkan; dan mereka yang hatinya keras merasa shalat berat, tetapi Jihad tetaplah yang paling menuntut dari semua tindakan ibadah secara absolut.

Inilah alasan mengapa beberapa orang berkata, seperti yang dikutip Al-Quran (yang artinya), "Ya Tuhan kami, mengapa Engkau wajibkan perang kepada kami? Hendaklah Engkau tangguhkan (kewajiban perang) kepada kami beberapa waktu lagi." [Surah An-Nisa' (4), Ayat 77.] Ini berarti bahwa setiap Muslim membutuhkan kesabaran untuk memaksa jiwanya kepada ketaatan, karena kesabaran diperlukan sebelum melaksanakan tindakan ketaatan apa pun, untuk membawa jiwa memulainya dan tulus dalam mendedikasikannya kepada Allah ﷻ. Juga diperlukan saat melakukan tindakan untuk membuat jiwa menyelesaikannya dengan sebaik mungkin. Juga diperlukan setelah menyelesaikan tindakan untuk mencegah hati mengagumi tindakan ketaatan tersebut dan lisan membicarakannya dengan sombong.

b. Sabar dalam menjauhi kemaksiatan kepada Allah ﷻ dan Rasul-Nya ﷺ: Tugas ini lebih sulit dan menuntut. Karena jiwa manusia, seperti yang dikatakan Al-Quran, cenderung kepada kejahatan dan menginginkannya, dan setan membujuk dan mendorong hamba untuk melakukan dosa. Mereka yang biasa melakukan perbuatan berdosa menyokong perbuatan itu dan mengundang orang-orang beriman untuk bergabung, sementara masyarakat tidak mencibir kemaksiatan atau mengkritik orang-orang yang berdosa. Sebaliknya, kadang-kadang masyarakat memuji orang-orang yang berdosa karena kerusakan umum yang ada di dalamnya. Jadi bagaimana anggota tubuh tidak menyerah pada seruan kemaksiatan? Memang, kesabaran itulah yang mencegah anggota tubuh dari melakukan dosa.

Kita pasti membutuhkan jenis kesabaran dan ketabahan yang ditunjukkan oleh Nabi Yusuf (as) ketika beliau menolak rayuan istri Al-Aziz, berkata (yang artinya), "Sesungguhnya barangsiapa yang bertakwa dan bersabar, maka sesungguhnya Allah tidak menyia-nyiakan pahala orang-orang yang berbuat baik." [Surah Yusuf (12), Ayat 90.] Jama'ah Muslim perlu mengajarkan jenis kesabaran dan ketabahan ini kepada anggotanya, karena kita pasti akan dikalahkan jika kita bermaksiat.

'Umar bin Al-Khaththab (ra) berbicara kebenaran dalam instruksinya kepada komandan tentara, Sa'd bin Abi Waqash (ra) ketika beliau berkata kepadanya, "Aku memerintahkan kamu dan tentaramu untuk bertakwa yang merupakan persiapan terbaik melawan musuh dan senjata perang yang paling kuat. Aku memerintahkan kamu dan tentaramu untuk lebih waspada terhadap dosa daripada terhadap musuh, karena dosa-dosa tentaramu harus lebih ditakuti daripada musuh. Sesungguhnya, kaum Muslimin hanya menang dengan dosa musuh mereka kepada Allah ﷻ, karena jika tidak kita tidak akan memiliki kekuatan dan keunggulan atas mereka. Jumlah kita tidak sebanding dengan mereka dan persiapan kita tidak menandingi mereka. Jika kita sama dengan mereka dalam dosa, mereka akan mengungguli kita melalui kekuatan fisik mereka. Jika kita tidak bisa mengalahkan mereka melalui ketakwaan kita, kita tidak akan mengalahkan mereka melalui kekuatan kita."

c. Sabar dalam menghadapi cobaan dan ujian: Adalah perlu bahwa setiap Muslim harus melaksanakan jenis kesabaran ini, karena jika tidak ia akan memberontak melawan Takdir Ilahi, dengan demikian melakukan dosa besar. "Apakah manusia itu mengira bahwa mereka dibiarkan (saja) mengatakan: 'Kami telah beriman', sedang mereka tidak diuji lagi? Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah menguji orang-orang yang sebelum mereka, maka sesungguhnya Allah mengetahui orang-orang yang benar dan sesungguhnya Dia mengetahui orang-orang yang dusta." [Surah Al-'Ankabut (29), Ayat 2-3.] Ujian-ujian ini penting untuk memisahkan yang baik dari yang buruk, dan membuang yang buruk dari barisan kafilah; kafilah orang-orang yang benar dan sabar.

Berkuasa di dunia ini bukanlah tugas yang mudah, karena hal itu menyiratkan pembawaan beban memimpin orang-orang dengan prinsip-prinsip agama Islam ini. Inilah mengapa hukum yang Allah ﷻ tetapkan di alam semesta mensyaratkan bahwa hanya mereka yang layak menduduki posisi kepemimpinan yang boleh memerintah. Ujian dan cobaan, oleh karena itu, sangat diperlukan untuk membedakan yang buruk dari yang baik. Mereka yang selamat dari ujian-ujian ini adalah mereka yang layak mendapat otoritas dan kekuasaan, "Allah sekali-kali tidak akan membiarkan orang-orang yang beriman dalam keadaan kamu sekarang ini, sehingga Dia menyisihkan yang buruk (munafik) dari yang baik (mukmin)." [Surah Ali Imran (3), Ayat 179.]

Jama'ah Muslim harus menyerukan kesabaran kepada anggotanya jika ia ingin layak mendapat kekuasaan. Siang dan malam ia harus menyerukan kepada mereka apa yang biasa Musa (as) serukan kepada kaumnya (yang artinya), "Mohonlah pertolongan kepada Allah dan bersabarlah; sesungguhnya bumi (ini) kepunyaan Allah; dipusakakan-Nya kepada siapa yang dikehendaki-Nya dari hamba-hamba-Nya. Dan kesudahan yang baik adalah bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa." [Surah Al-A'raf (7), Ayat 128.] Jama'ah Muslim harus mengajarkan mereka demikian, "Atau apakah kamu mengira bahwa kamu akan masuk surga, padahal belum datang kepadamu (cobaan) sebagaimana halnya orang-orang terdahulu sebelum kamu? Mereka ditimpa oleh malapetaka dan kesengsaraan, serta digoncangkan (dengan berbagai cobaan) sehingga berkatalah Rasul dan orang-orang yang beriman bersamanya: 'Bilakah datangnya pertolongan Allah?' Ingatlah, sesungguhnya pertolongan Allah itu amat dekat." [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayat 214.] Ia harus membesarkan mereka menjadi orang-orang sejati, sehingga ketika masa-masa ujian datang, mereka berkata apa yang dikatakan orang-orang sejati (yang artinya), "Inilah yang dijanjikan Allah dan Rasul-Nya kepada kami dan Allah dan Rasul-Nya telah berkata benar." [Surah Al-Ahzab (33), Ayat 22.] Hanya orang-orang seperti itu yang dianggap layak untuk mengemban amanah iman ini. Musuh-musuh mereka akan segera disingkirkan sebagaimana api membuang kotoran dari logam yang dilebur. Bersabar dengan kesulitan dan bencana memerlukan hal-hal berikut:
content_ar:
Any group of people who want to work for Islam must have their share and provision of endurance. Otherwise, they should leave this path to those who are more deserved of it, namely the truthful and patient believers. In his biography of النبي ﷺ , Ibn Kathir wrote, " رسول الله ﷺ spent ten years in Makkah, preaching to the people in their homes and in public gatherings and saying to them, 'Who would shelter me and support me until I convey the Message of الله, and [such a person] will receive the [reward of) Paradise.' But he did not find anyone to shelter and support him, until the Ansaar (or the helpers) believed him and embraced Islam. Seventy-two of them came to meet him at a place known as Al- 'Aqabah. النبي ﷺ promised them Paradise if they accepted his conditions.

The youngest of them, As'ad bin Zurarah, stood up and said, 'Slow down, O people of Yathrib 126. We did not tire our camels [on the way here] for him without knowing that he is رسول الله ﷺ . Supporting him today will be in defiance of all the Arabs, it will result in the killing of your best men and the blow of swords upon you. Either you endure this, in which case take his offer and expect your reward from الله, or if you are unsure of yourselves and afraid, in which case abandon him to his fate. So tell him your choice now, for either way he will vouch for you before الله.

' They said, `Stand aside O As'ad! By الله, we will never leave this allegiance to him, nor will we cancel it.'" The Muslim Jama'ah that wishes to support this faith and aspires after Paradise by doing so must have sufficient endurance to enable it to fulfil this commitment, assume the responsibility and confront the enemies. If, however, it does not know what endurance is, cannot stand what it entails and has no intention to exercise it, it must admit to its failings and step aside. Doing so might excuse it before الله and is better for Islam than going forth carrying the banner and then soon turning on its heels, drooping the banner, only to cause great tumult and oppression in the land. رسول الله ﷺ said, "And know that victory comes with endurance." 127 So whoever wants victory must show patience and endurance.

الله (SWT) says (which means), "And the fair promise of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel because they had patience and constancy." [Surah Al-A'raaf (7), Ayah 137.] 126 Yathrib was the name of the city which later came to known as Al-Madeenah. 127 Tirmidhi. 125 guidance under Our Command, so long as they persevered with patience and continued to have faith in Our signs." [Surah As-Sajdah (32), Ayah 24.] The Muslim Jama'ah, therefore, must teach endurance to its members, train them and cloak them with it. النبي ﷺ said, "He who tries to endure is assisted by الله in his endeavour." The Jama'ah must teach its members that endurance is a vehicle that must be alighted in order for them to attain the honour and success that are the dream and aspiration of the Muslim Ummah. It must also teach them that endurance is the conduit to all the degrees and stations of faith. Those who cannot endure will not achieve anywhere. Whoever wants to acquire some knowledge must patiently learn and memorise.

Whoever aspires to the higher stations of piety will not achieve this aim without patience and endurance. Likewise, those who wish to rid themselves of sinful habits and conduct and cleanse their hearts and limbs, need patience and endurance to carry them through this process. Indeed, patience and endurance is the key to all good and the light of all guidance. الله (SWT) says (which means), "As for those who wage Jihad in Our Cause, We will surely guide them to Our Paths (i. e. الله's religion).

And verily, الله is with those who do right." [Surah Al-'Ankabut (29), Ayah 69.] Furthermore, maintaining any of the stations thus achieved requires additional endurance. Those who cease to strive and abandon endurance will be stripped of their gains, their status will be reduced and their progress reversed. The need for patience and endurance is especially important for those who work in the cause of الله. Preachers need patient perseverance so as not to lose hope when they see that no-one has responded to their call. They also need to endure any reaction to their activities that might be harmful to them.

Educationalists need patient perseverance to deal with the mistakes and inattention of their charges. The leaders need it to lead those who are not inclined to obedience. Those who undertake enjoining good and forbidding evil need it to I endure harm. And those who undertake Jihad are without doubt most in need for endurance in order to keep up the struggle under all conditions and in all circumstances, "Fighting [Jihad] is ordained upon you though you dislike it." [Surah AlBaqarah (2), Ayah 216.] Endurance is more needed today than ever before. We live with our dawah in countries controlled by our enemies whom we aim to remove and replace their rule with that of our Shariah.

They will frequently attack us and seek to destroy us, and without endurance they will undoubtedly succeed in uprooting our dawah and wiping us off the face of this earth. Endurance is not what many people today think it is, namely not speaking up for the truth and making compromises with falsehood. This is in fact no more than humiliation, degradation and disgrace, and has nothing to do with endurance. The endurance which our Shariah commands us to observe is of three categories. a. Endurance in obeying الله and His Messenger : While the self inclines to comfort and inaction, religious obligations entail a degree of hardship that varies from one commission to the next.

Jihad, for instance, 126 making wudu' with cold water in winter is harder than making it in summer. The degree of effort also varies from one individual to the other: those who are closer to their homes and families find emigration extremely taxing; and those with a hardened heart find prayers laborious, but Jihad remains the most exacting of absolutely all acts of worship. This is the reason why some said, as the القرآن الكريم recounts (which means), "Our Lord! Why have You ordered for us fighting? Would that You had granted us respite for a short period?" [Surah An-Nisa (4), Ayah 77.] This means that every Muslim needs endurance to compel his self into obedience, for patience is needed before performing any act of obedience, to bring the self to start it and be sincere in devoting it to الله.

It is also needed while doing the act to make the self see it through in the best possible way. It is also needed after completion of the act to prevent the heart from admiring the act of obedience and the tongue from boastfully talking about it. b. Endurance in avoiding the disobedience of الله and His Messenger : This task is more difficult and demanding. For the human self, as the القرآن الكريم said, is inclined to evil and wishes for it, and Satan entices and encourages the servant to commit the sin. Those who habitually indulge in sinful deeds advocate them and invite the faithful to join in, while society does not frown upon vice nor criticise the sinful.

To the contrary, it sometimes praises the sinful because of the general corruption in which it wallows. So how can the limbs give succumb to the call of disobedience? Indeed, it is endurance which prevents the limbs from committing the sin. We definitely need the type of patience and endurance showed by Prophet Yusuf when he resisted the advances of Al-Aziz's wife, saying (which means), "Verily, he who fears الله with obedience to Him and is patient, then surely, الله makes not the reward of those who do right to be lost." [Surah Yusuf (12), Ayah 90.] The Muslim Jama'ah is in need of teaching this type of patience and endurance to its members, for we will surely be defeated if we are disobedient. 'Umar bin AlKhattab spoke the truth in his instructions to the army commander, Sa'd bin Abee Waqqas when he said to him, "I command you and your soldiers to observe Taqwa which is the best preparation against the enemy and the strongest weapon of war.

I command you and your soldiers to be more on guard against sins than against the enemy, for the sins of [your] army should be feared more than the enemy. Indeed, Muslims only triumph by the sinning of their enemy against الله, for otherwise we would not have the strength and advantage over them. Our numbers are no match for them and our preparation do not compare. If we equal them in sins, they will prevail over us through their physical strength. If we cannot defeat them through our piety, we will not defeat them through our strength." He also said.

"And do not say that our enemy is more evil than we are, and that الله will not send them against us even if we sin. For so many are those against whom الله sent a people who are more evil than them, as happened with the Jews when الله (SWT) sent against them the disbelievers from amongst the Magians who entered the very innermost parts of their homes, and it was a promise completely fulfilled." 127 and persevere in their resistance to the inequity and oppression of people, and how to detest and abhor this evil quality. For if they do not learn to dissociate themselves from injustice, those of them who might, in the future, be in positions of power and responsibility might then succumb to the temptation of oppressing others. Another great vice and abhorrent act of disobedience which members of the Muslim Jama'ah must avoid is the love of leadership and influence, for so often has the struggle over leadership split the Muslim community and weakened its resolve. c. Enduring trials and tribulations: It is necessary that every Muslim must exercise this type of endurance, for otherwise he will rebel against the Divine Destiny, thus committing a major sin, "Do people think that they will be left alone because they say, 'We believe', and that they will not be tested?

And We indeed tested those who were before them. And الله will certainly make fit] known [the truth of] those who are true, and will certainly make [it] known [the falsehood of] those who are liars [although الله knows all that before putting them to test]." [Surah Al-'Ankabut (29), Ayah 2-3.] These tests are important for separating between the good and the bad, and casting the bad away from the ranks of the caravan; the caravan of the people of truth and endurance. Prevailing in this life is no easy task, for it implies the carrying of the burden of ruling the people by the tenets of this religion of Islam. ' This is why the laws that الله established in the universe stipulates that only those entitled to occupy the positions of leadership should be allowed to govern. Trials and tribulations are, therefore, indispensable to distinguish the wicked from the good.

Those who survive these tests are those worthy of authority and power, "الله will not leave the believers in the state in which you are now, until He distinguishes the wicked from the good." [Surah Ale-Imran (3), Ayah 179.] "In order that الله may distinguish the wicked (disbelievers, polytheists, evil-doers) from the good (the believers), put the wicked one on another, heap them together, and cast them into Hell." [Surah Al-Anfal (8), Ayah 37.] The Muslim Jama'ah must preach endurance to its members if it is to deserve power. Day and night it must preach to them what Moses used to preach to his people (which means), "Seek help in الله and be patient. Verily, the earth is الله's. He gives it as a heritage to Whom He will of His servants, and the [blessed] end is (best) for the righteous." [Surah Al-A'raaf (7), Ayah 128.] The Muslim Jama'ah must teach them thus, "Or think you that you will enter Paradise without such [trials] as came to those who passed away before you? They were afflicted with severe poverty and ailments and were so shaken that even the Messenger and those 128 Certainly, the help of الله is near!" [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayah 214.] It must rear them to be true men, so that when the testing times come, they say what true men say (which means), "This is what الله and His Messenger had promised us, and الله and His Messenger had spoken the truth." [Surah Al-Ahzaab (33), Ayah 22.] Only such people are deemed qualified to carry the trust of this faith.

Their enemies will soon be discharged as fire discharges impurities from smelted metal. Patiently enduring hardships and calamities require the following:
